HMAC Digest precalculation (gxcr/tokens/hmac_precalc.h)
[Crypto API (gxcr/gxcr.h)]

Detailed Description

The HMAC precalculation token is used to hardware-accelerate a necessary initialization step on a context record before it can be used by the Packet Processing engine to calculate a digest in HMAC mode.

When calculating an HMAC digest, the hardware expects that the HMAC key has been transformed into an inner and an outer digest and placed in the appropriate fields of the context record. This token is used internallly by the gxcr functions to generate those values, but it can also be used by a custom function to perform this initialization step. Many higher-level protocols, such as IPsec, can use HMAC mode for digest calculations.

Functions

int hmac_precalc_setup (void *metadata_mem, int metadata_mem_len, int hash_algorithm, int finish_digest, int digest_size, int data_size)
 Function to set up the token and hardware context record for calculating HMAC inner and outer digests.
void hmac_precalc_process_start (gxio_mica_context_t *mica_context, void *metadata_mem, int metadata_mem_len, void *data_mem, int data_mem_len)
 Function to perform the precalculation step.
void hmac_precalc_process (gxio_mica_context_t *mica_context, void *metadata_mem, int metadata_mem_len, void *data_mem, int data_mem_len)
void * hmac_precalc_digest (void *metadata_mem)

Function Documentation

int hmac_precalc_setup ( void *  metadata_mem,
int  metadata_mem_len,
int  hash_algorithm,
int  finish_digest,
int  digest_size,
int  data_size 
)

Function to set up the token and hardware context record for calculating HMAC inner and outer digests.

Parameters:
metadata_mem Pointer to memory that has been registered with the MiCA shim via gxio_mica_register_page().
metadata_mem_len Length, in bytes, of provided metadata memory.
hash_algorithm The digest algorithm to use. This value is obtained from the hash_algorithm field of the gxcr_context_control_words_t of the initialized gxcr_context_t.
finish_digest if 1, run a complete digest (useful for reducing a long key). if 0, leave digest incomplete (used when computing an inner or outer digest from a key).
digest_size Size, in bytes, of digest produced by this algorithm.
data_size Size, in bytes, of the data to be processed in this step. This will be the HMAC key length if this is a key-reduction step, or the digest block size if this is an inner or outer precalculation.

void hmac_precalc_process_start ( gxio_mica_context_t *  mica_context,
void *  metadata_mem,
int  metadata_mem_len,
void *  data_mem,
int  data_mem_len 
)

Function to perform the precalculation step.

This function schedules the operation with the MiCA shim and returns immediately. The function gxio_mica_is_busy() may be called to poll for completion.

Parameters:
mica_context An initalized MiCA context.
metadata_mem Pointer to memory that has been registered with the MiCA shim via gxio_mica_register_page(), and has been initialized by the function hmac_precalc_setup.
metadata_mem_len Length, in bytes, of provided metadata memory.
data_mem Pointer to the data to be processed. The memory must be registered with the MiCA shim via gxio_mica_register_page().
data_mem_len Length, in bytes, of the data to be processed. The returned data, written to data_mem, will be a multiple of 4 bytes, so the actual memory allocated may need to be up to 3 bytes larger than the processed amount.

void hmac_precalc_process ( gxio_mica_context_t *  mica_context,
void *  metadata_mem,
int  metadata_mem_len,
void *  data_mem,
int  data_mem_len 
)

Parameters:
mica_context An initalized MiCA context.
metadata_mem Pointer to memory that has been registered with the MiCA shim via gxio_mica_register_page(), and has been initialized by the function hmac_precalc_setup.
metadata_mem_len Length, in bytes, of provided metadata memory.
data_mem Pointer to the data to be processed. The memory must be registered with the MiCA shim via gxio_mica_register_page().
data_mem_len Length, in bytes, of the data to be processed. The returned data, written to data_mem, will be a multiple of 4 bytes, so the actual memory allocated may need to be up to 3 bytes larger than the processed amount.

void* hmac_precalc_digest ( void *  metadata_mem  ) 

Parameters:
metadata_mem Pointer to metadata memory that has been processed by hmac_precalc_process() or hmac_precalc_process_start(). Returns a pointer to the calculated digest value.
